rulings.Case Title: EASTON RUHNKE ET AL VS. HUBBELL POWER SYSTEMS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Tuesday, April 1, 2025, Line 1. DEFENDANT PRO ENERGY SERVICES GROUP, LLC's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. No party having opposed the motion, the motion of defendant Pro Energy Services Group, LLC for a determination of good faith settlement is granted and the court finds a good faith settlement pursuant to CCP section 877.6. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN KING VS. HOTEL EPIK, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action Of Plaintiff Jonathan King As Terminating Sanction For Continued Failure To Respond To Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,635.00; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Christine D. Luong-Pham
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Tuesday, April 1, 2025, Line 3. DEFENDANT NEW PACIFIC HOTEL INC. DBA HOTEL EPIK's Motion To Dismiss Action Of Plaintiff Jonathan King As Terminating Sanction For Continued Failure To Respond To Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,635.00. The unopposed motion of defendant New Pacific Hotel Inc. for terminating sanctions in view of Plaintiff's failure to respond to discovery is granted. On October 2, 2024, the court ordered Plaintiff to respond to outstanding discovery. The moving papers establish that he has not yet done so and trial is set for May 5, 2025. Monetary sanctions have been ineffective. The court grants the motion for terminating sanctions and dismisses the matter pursuant to CCP 2023.030(d)(3). This is a personal injury action and Defendant cannot reasonably be expected to defend it without discovery from Plaintiff about his claims and damages. In light of its terminating sanction, the court does not order further monetary sanctions to Defendant. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Seal Records Conditionally Filed Under Seal (Amended)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Tuesday, April 1, 2025, Line 4. 2 - PLAINTIFF ENERSPONSE, LLC's Motion To Seal Records Conditionally Filed Under Seal. The unopposed motion of Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Enersponse, LLC ("Enersponse") for an order maintaining under seal certain portions of Defendant/Cross-Complainant SmartRent Technologies, Inc.'s ("SmartRent") Motion for Summary Judgment ("Motion"), specifically portions of SmartRent's: (1) Memorandum of Points and Authorities in support of the Motion ("Memorandum"); (2) Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts in support of the Motion ("Separate Statement"); and (3) Declaration of James Maxwell Cooper in support of the Motion, including Exhibits G - M, O, and P attached thereto, and Exhibit Nos. 49, 50, 51 to James McPhail's deposition (Exhibit P) (collectively, the"Confidential Exhibits") is granted. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely.
